title: default mode, no rules -> every write-type tool call is ask, regardless of path or tool

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/a-default-deny/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: Permission required to write the file. Please approve when ready.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p1a-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: Permission denied for writing outside the working directory.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/a-default-deny/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

First use the Read tool to read $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/a-default-deny/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/a-default-deny/note.txt. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool calls.
probes.result_text: Permission required to edit the file. The permission system is blocking the write operation.

probes.prompt: Use the Read tool to read the file $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/a-default-deny/sentinel.txt and output its exact contents. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain.

hypothesis: In `default` mode with no rules, the permission engine returns ask for every write-type tool call — independent of target path (cwd / $HOME / subdir) and of tool (Write / Edit). The classic 'denyしてないのに拒否される' is this ask left unapproved (headless auto-deny). Positive control (read-cwd): read-only tools sit in the no-approval tier, so default denies nothing but writes — Read succeeds with no prompt even in headless.
